item.title: Intellia CRISPR Therapy
item.description:
• Summary: Intellia Therapeutics reported a landmark Phase 3 success for its in vivo CRISPR therapy (NTLA-2002) in hereditary angioedema (HAE), marking the first late-stage win for an in vivo gene editor. The data showed significant reduction in attack rates, leading to plans for FDA submission. The company also announced a partnership with Eli Lilly to develop the therapy, further validating the platform. Analysts view this as a pivotal moment for CRISPR-based medicines.
  • MoAs: In vivo CRISPR/Cas9 gene editing to knock out the KLKB1 gene, reducing prekallikrein levels and bradykinin production, thereby preventing HAE attacks.
  • Sources: Endpoints News, BioPharma Dive, Fierce Biotech, BioSpace, STAT News, STAT News, Endpoints News

item.title: Gene Therapy Regulatory Models for Baby KJs
item.description:
• Summary: Gene therapy leaders are proposing novel regulatory models to help children with Krabbe disease (often referred to as 'Baby KJs') gain faster access to potentially life-saving gene therapies. The initiative aims to streamline development and approval pathways for these targeted treatments, addressing the urgent need for therapies in this rare and devastating condition.
  • MoAs: Gene replacement therapy for galactocerebrosidase (GALC) deficiency (mechanism not specified if using AAV or other vector).
  • Sources: BioSpace
